channel.title: NYT > World > Middle East
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: U.S. Military Presence in Jordan Makes It Iranian Target
item.description: Relying heavily on U.S. aid and protection, Jordan is unlikely to restrict the American military presence there, despite escalating Iranian airstrikes.
item.dc:creator: Raja Abdulrahim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: American and Jordanian soldiers participating in a military exercise northeast of Amman in 2022.

item.title: Diesel Crisis Puts Poor Farmers Around the World at Risk
item.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has limited the shipment of diesel, depriving farmers of fuel for irrigation pumps and threatening the food supply.
item.dc:creator: Peter S. Goodman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Fabeha Monir for The New York Times

item.title: Debate Over Ancient Iranian Jewelry Worn by Zendaya to ‘Odyssey’ Event
item.description: The actress wore earrings to an event for “The Odyssey” that a London gallery has said were made from an ancient artifact.
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi
item.media:credit: Scott A Garfitt/Invision, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Zendaya at an event for “The Odyssey” in London this month.

item.title: Houthis Claim Strikes on 2 Saudi Oil Tankers in Red Sea
item.description: The Saudi authorities confirmed a strike on one of the ships. These are the first claimed attacks since the Iranian-backed Yemeni group announced a blockade on Saudi Arabia.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A satellite image of the Bab al-Mandab Strait earlier this month.

item.title: With Saudi Deal, Trump Loosens Nuclear Standards for One Close Ally
item.description: Nuclear experts say it throws fuel on the smoldering fire of atomic proliferation, creating a destabilizing situation that could raise the risk of nuclear war.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger and William J. Broad
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
Abdulaziz bin Salman, Prince of Saudi Arabia
item.media:description: President Trump with Mohammed bin Salman, the crown prince of Saudi Arabia, at the White House last year.

item.title: What Visuals Show About Recent Damage to U.S. Military Sites in the Middle East
item.description: Video footage and satellite images reveal new details about Iranian strikes on U.S. targets during the latest flare-up in fighting.
item.dc:creator: Aric Toler and Christoph Koettl
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: U.S. Troops, Seriously Wounded in Recent Strikes, Were Flown to Germany for Help
item.description: The seriously wounded service members are among the roughly 100 American troops that the Defense Department recently said were injured by Iranian attacks since early July.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Donald Trump, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and other U.S. officials attending the arrival of U.S. Army soldiers killed in recent Iranian strikes.

item.title: Israeli Banks Threaten to Cut Off Palestinian Economy
item.description: Two Israeli financial firms have long served as partners to Palestinian banks, handling transactions that make possible the West Bank’s imports and exports.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger and Adam Rasgon
item.media:credit: Simon Beni/Middle East Images, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: The Bank Hapoalim tower in Tel Aviv. Bank Hapoalim and Israel Discount Bank have written to their Palestinian counterparts saying they will end banking relationships with them.

item.title: How the Trump Administration’s Nuclear Deal With Saudi Arabia May Work
item.description: The Trump administration announced an agreement with Saudi Arabia on a civilian nuclear program. Such deals, known as 123 Agreements, are designed to prevent the development of weapons.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel
item.media:description: President Trump with the Saudi crown prince, Mohammed bin Salman, in Washington last year.

item.title: Coveted U.S. Nuclear Deal Is a Coup for Saudi Crown Prince
item.description: Saudi Arabia has for years sought a deal to obtain American nuclear technology. The agreement, signed on Wednesday, could deepen U.S.-Saudi ties for decades to come.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prince Mohammed bin Salman, the de facto Saudi ruler, and President Trump at the White House in November.

item.title: U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal Stokes Israeli Concerns of Mideast Arms Race
item.description: An Israeli minister sought to downplay the agreement, while critics said it could spur other regional countries to pursue a nuclear program, and potentially the creation of a bomb.
item.dc:creator: Natan Odenheimer and Aaron Boxerman
item.media:description: Nir Barkat, Israel’s minister of economy, in Tel Aviv last year. He has been the most senior Israeli government official to comment so far on the expected U.S.-Saudi nuclear deal.

item.title: Global Oil Price Jumps as War Threats Intensify
item.description: But stock investors shrug off Iran developments as they await crucial earnings reports from tech firms including Alphabet and Tesla.
item.dc:creator: Laura Stevens and Jason Karaian
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tomas Munita for The New York Times

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Shirin Hakim, Alan Yuhas and Pranav Baskar

item.title: See How Houthis Put the Red Sea at Risk as an Alternative Oil Route
item.description: Saudi Arabia has diverted huge amounts of oil to the Red Sea since the Iran war began, but now the Iranian-backed Houthi militants say that they intend to block that route.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott, Lazaro Gamio and Samuel Granados
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Trump Lifts Ban on Direct Flights to Lebanon
item.description: The prohibition had been in place since 1985, when it was imposed in response to Hezbollah’s hijacking of TWA Flight 847.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump with President Joseph Aoun of Lebanon at the White House on Tuesday.

item.title: With His Latest Threats, Trump Signals to Iran That He Is Willing to Escalate
item.description: President Trump is facing a critical moment in the war, which has failed to bring about the “unconditional surrender” he once demanded.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: “We’ll be hitting that area probably pretty soon,” President Trump said of Pickaxe Mountain on Tuesday. “And there’s not a thing they can do about it.”

item.title: Trump Threatened to Strike Pickaxe Mountain, a Suspected Iranian Nuclear Site. Here’s What to Know.
item.description: The fortified underground complex, in central Iran, is about one mile away from the Natanz uranium enrichment facility.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: Institute for Science and International Security
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A satellite image provided by Vantor shows an entrance to tunnels at Pickaxe Mountain in Iran last month.

item.title: Water Plants Under Fire in the Gulf Are Crucial to Civilians
item.description: Several days of airstrikes have hit water and energy plants in Kuwait, a nation that relies on desalination for 90 percent of its drinking water.
item.dc:creator: Zane Irwin
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Airbus DS SPOT CNES, via Reuters
item.media:description: A satellite image showing smoke rising from the Subiya Power and Water Desalination Plant in Kuwait on Saturday.

item.title: Netanyahu Ad Uses a Questionable Tactic Against Bereaved Rival
item.description: A.I.-generated images showing Gadi Eisenkot, the Israeli leader’s top challenger in upcoming elections, played on anti-Arab sentiments and used images seen as disrespectful to his son, who was killed in the recent Gaza war.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger
item.media:description: Gadi Eisenkot, leader of the Yashar party, speaking at the launch of his party’s election campaign in Drom HaSharon, Israel, last month.

item.title: Iran’s top diplomat says ‘security gap’ may have led to strikes on supreme leader.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Abbas Araghchi, Iran’s foreign minister, during a news conference in New Delhi in May.

item.title: Lebanese Army Accuses Israeli Troops of Firing as Its Forces Deploy in ‘Pilot Zone’
item.description: The action raised tensions as the sides were taking their first steps under a U.S.-brokered deal to transfer control of occupied areas to Lebanon after months of war.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Lebanese army troops in Zawtar al-Gharbiya, southern Lebanon, after Israeli forces withdrew from the area on Tuesday.

item.title: Houthis in Yemen Edge Closer to Entering U.S.-Iran War
item.description: The Iran-backed Houthi militia in Yemen has declared a Red Sea blockade on Saudi Arabia, potentially opening a new front in the war rippling across the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Ismaeel Naar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Yemeni supporters of the Iran-backed Houthi movement gather during a rally against what the group says are restrictions imposed by the Saudi-led coalition on Houthi-controlled areas of Yemen, in Sana last week.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Max Bearak, Jenny Gross, John Ismay and Catie Edmondson

item.title: Trump Says Netanyahu Will Not Be Arrested in the U.S.
item.description: New York’s mayor, Zohran Mamdani, told The New York Times, he was looking into whether he could arrest Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu when he comes to New York for the U.N. General Assembly.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:description: Israel’s ambassador to the United Nations, Danny Danon, accused New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani of engaging in “political theater” over his comments about arresting Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.

item.title: Inside the Deadly U.S. Airstrike on a Shadow Fleet Tanker
item.description: Evidence suggests the ship on which three Indian sailors died was not moving when it was struck in the Gulf of Oman, but it had been engaged in risky offshore transfers of oil.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar, Pragati K.B. and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Suspected Iranian strikes have targeted the U.S. military in at least 4 countries.
item.dc:creator: Sanjana Varghese, Samuel Granados, Riley Mellen and Aric Toler

item.title: Hamas Names Khalil al-Hayya as Its New Leader
item.description: Seen as closer to Iran, Khalil al-Hayya served as the Palestinian militant group’s chief negotiator with Israel and the Trump administration during the Gaza war.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman and Adam Rasgon
item.media:description: Khalil al-Hayya speaking with reporters in Istanbul in 2024.

item.title: Houthis in Yemen Declare Red Sea Blockade on Saudi Arabia
item.description: The Iran-backed Houthi militia, which controls parts of Yemen, has in the past blockaded the Red Sea by threatening a choke point crucial to Saudi oil exports and global shipping.
item.dc:creator: Ismaeel Naar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Pentagon Identifies 2 U.S. Soldiers Killed in Jordan During Iran Strike
item.description: Both were active-duty soldiers, the Defense Department said. They were killed in action after an Iranian strike on a base in Jordan on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Jacey Fortin, Ruth Graham, Maia Spoto and Sean Keenan
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Pentagon has confirmed the deaths of 17 U.S. service members in the war with Iran. Another military member is missing after an airstrike in Jordan.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Ismaeel Naar and Sanam Mahoozi

item.title: U.S. Average Gas Price Returns to $4 a Gallon as Iran Crisis Escalates
item.description: A month ago, drivers were enjoying some relief at the pump after the U.S. and Iran signed a deal intended to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Now, prices are rising again.
item.dc:creator: Katie Robertson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Gabriel V. Cárdenas for The New York Times
item.media:description: Gasoline prices, which had been falling in recent weeks, are almost back up to where they were a month ago.

item.title: Rubio Says That U.S. Is Still Open to Diplomacy With Iran
item.description: Iranian foreign ministry officials and Secretary of State Marco Rubio signaled openness to resume negotiations but declined to provide any new details.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Brendan Smialowski/Agence France-Presse, Pool/AFP via Getty Images
item.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio speaking to reporters at Joint Base Andrews in Maryland on Sunday night.

item.title: Trump Administration and Iran Officials Risk a Wider War They Cannot Afford
item.description: U.S. and Iranian attacks are unfolding in a way that risks a wider war they cannot contain, analysts warn, unless they can return to negotiations quickly.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Iranians, like these people walking under a poster of the country’s three supreme leaders, are growing increasingly frustrated with the war.

item.title: Oil Markets on Edge as Houthi Rebels Threaten Shipping in the Middle East
item.description: The global oil price went above $90 but pulled back on signs of hope for a reduction in tensions.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Iran shipped billions in oil during the cease-fire.
item.dc:creator: Stacy Cowley
item.media:description: Iran capitalized on its export of oil, gaining billions of dollars during a short-lived peace agreement with the United States.

item.title: Israel watches tensely as the U.S.-Iran fighting escalates.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.media:description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has vowed to continue the military campaign against Iran.

item.title: The U.S. Is Sending More Warplanes to the Middle East, Officials Say
item.description: F-16 and F-35 jets were en route to the region from Europe, according to U.S. officials who were not authorized to speak publicly.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Fighter jets stationed in Germany and Britain are being deployed to the Middle East according to officials.

item.title: Increased Cooperation With U.S. Has Put Jordan in Iran’s Cross Hairs
item.description: Jordan’s role in U.S. operations against Iran has grown as other allies in the region have restricted Washington’s ability to use their bases and airspace.
item.dc:creator: Raja Abdulrahim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Iran’s recent attacks on U.S.-allied Jordan, including one that killed two U.S. service members on Friday, introduce a new complexity in the war.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Yan Zhuang, Hari Raj and Eric Schmitt

item.title: A Modest Proposal for the Strait Dispute: A Shared Pact Among Gulf States
item.description: A London-based research group has invoked a now-defunct European pact on coal and steel to suggest forming a coalition of countries that would share control of the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Oil tankers and cargo vessels were anchored last month off the coast of Oman during an effective blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Why Jordan Is Becoming a New Focus in the U.S.-Iran War
item.description: Four Iranian attacks in five days have targeted U.S. troops in Jordan, U.S. officials said, including one on Friday that killed two soldiers and left another service member missing.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe, Julian E. Barnes and Jonathan Swan
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: American and Jordanian forces took part in multinational military exercises hosted by Jordan in 2022.

item.title: Deaths of 2 U.S. Troops in Jordan Brings American Toll in the War With Iran to 16
item.description: President Trump has vowed to avenge the deaths of any U.S. military personnel.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay and Lara Jakes
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The remains of a U.S. service member killed in Kuwait earlier in the war arrived at Dover Air Force Base, Del., in March.

item.title: U.S. Strikes Leave Iranians Isolated and Scared
item.description: Overnight strikes damaged a tunnel and three bridges in Hormozgan Province, the governor’s office said.
item.dc:creator: Leily Nikounazar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Persian Gulf off the coast of Bandar Abbas, an Iranian port city in the south of the country, in May. Residents said roads around the city were heavily damaged by U.S. airstrikes this week.

item.title: Kuwait accuses Iran of targeting civilian sites after attacks spark fires.
item.dc:creator: Ismaeel Naar and Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay, Aaron Boxerman, Leily Nikounazar and Yan Zhuang

item.title: Israel Counters Iranian Spying by Warning Against Recruitment
item.description: The Israeli authorities have enacted a broad array of countermeasures to contain what they describe as online recruiting efforts by Iranian agents.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: People walking in Tehran this month. According to Israeli authorities, Iran has intensified efforts to recruit people in Israel for intelligence gathering.
